title: peltogyne parvifolia subsp. parvifolia
intro: Peltogyne parvifolia subsp. parvifolia, also known as the small-leaved Peltogyne, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a tree that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The bark of the Peltogyne parvifolia subsp. parvifolia is rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and colds, and may also help to support the immune system and promote overall health.
